skills.name: Build Custom Apps
skills.description: Build, modify, and debug experimental Omnideck Custom Apps with native web frontends, optional Python actions, persistent data, and chat integration.
skills.prompt: # Build Omnideck Custom Apps Create or edit Custom Apps in `/home/omnideck/apps/`. Use a lowercase slug containing only letters, numbers, and hyphens. Directories and directory symlinks are supported; valid apps are discovered automatically. ## Structure ```text / omnideck.json web/ index.html app.py # optional data/ # persistent app-owned data ``` Use this manifest shape: ```json { "title": "My App", "description": "What it does", "icon": "bi-window" } ``` ## Frontend Use native HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Multiple files and nested folders are supported; no bundler is required. Keep the outer app document on its entry page and use hash routing for client-side routes. To show files in IDE-style tabs, render them inside the app with `fetch`, media elements, or nested iframes; Omnideck restores the app if its outer frame navigates away. Custom Apps are trusted, same-origin, unsandboxed pages with normal browser capabilities. Namespace browser storage keys per app. Load the SDK: ```html ``` Call a Python action: ```js const result = await window.omnideck.invoke("greet", { name: "Ada" }); ``` Open the active chat beside the app, or seed its composer without sending: ```js window.omnideck.chat.open(); window.omnideck.chat.compose({ text: "Help me with this", context: result, }); ``` The SDK exposes only `invoke`, `chat.open`, and `chat.compose`. Use normal browser APIs for fetch, storage, downloads, media, and external resources. Frontend files are served with `Cache-Control: no-store`, but an already open app must be reloaded or reopened to display changes. Backend changes apply on the next action call. ## Backend Define callable actions in `app.py`: ```python from custom_apps import action @action def greet(name: str): return {"message": f"Hello, {name}!"} ``` Only decorated functions are callable. Actions may be synchronous or asynchronous. Arguments and results must be JSON-compatible. Each call runs in a fresh Python process with a 120-second timeout, filesystem and network access, and no persistent memory, streaming, progress events, or background worker. Prefer the standard library and packages already available in the Omnideck environment. Store persistent app-owned state in `/data/`, locating it relative to `app.py`; never hardcode the resolved installation path. Use files or SQLite for persistence. ## User files Existing files under `/home/omnideck` are browser-addressable at matching URLs: ```html ``` ```js const text = await fetch("/home/omnideck/notes/example.txt") .then((response) => response.text()); ``` `GET` reads existing files. `PUT` overwrites existing files and must include `X-Requested-With: XMLHttpRequest`. Use Python actions to list directories or create, rename, and delete files. Validate user-provided paths and do not expose secrets in errors. Use `/home/omnideck` for files meant to be shared with the user, other apps, or loaded directly by the frontend. Keep private app state in `/data/`. After building or changing an app, validate its manifest and structure, exercise important Python actions, and tell the user to refresh or reopen the Custom App if it is already open.
